terms.definition: A simple robot that executes primitive commands in a grid world.
terms.example: public class MyKarel extends Karel { public void run() { move(); } }

terms.definition: A grid of streets (rows) and avenues (columns) containing walls and beepers.
terms.example: // Karel starts at 1st Street, 1st Avenue, facing East by default (typical).

terms.definition: A horizontal row in Karel’s world; street numbers increase northward.
A vertical column in Karel’s world; avenue numbers increase eastward.
terms.example:
// Move east along a street:
while (frontIsClear()) { move(); }

terms.definition: An object Karel can place on or pick up from a corner.
An impassable barrier that blocks Karel’s forward movement.
Moves Karel forward one corner in the direction it is facing; errors if blocked.
Rotates Karel 90 degrees to the left.
terms.example: turnLeft(); // face north if starting east

terms.definition: Rotates Karel 90 degrees to the right (available in SuperKarel or via helper).
terms.example:
// In base Karel, implement:
private void turnRight() { turnLeft(); turnLeft(); turnLeft(); }

terms.definition: Places one beeper from Karel’s bag onto the current corner.
Picks up one beeper from the current corner into Karel’s bag (if present).
True if there is no wall directly in front of Karel.
True if one or more beepers are on Karel’s current corner.
Repeatedly executes a block of code while a boolean condition remains true.
Chooses between two code paths based on a boolean condition.
